reasoning: I have wrestled with this for months, and even now my hand hesitates on the button. Biswajit Das is unprincipled and TMC's corruption—Sandeshkhali, the SSC scam, everything—makes me sick with genuine anger. But I am not voting for him or for them. I am voting against a Centre that has shown it will weaponize every agency, every arrest, every case to remake Bengal into something we are not. Yes, Mamata's government is rotten. But Delhi's justice is not blind—it is selective, timed, saturated exactly when elections approach. If I vote for Shantanu, I am voting for the CAA that I have warned about since 2019, for Hindutva repackaged as Hindu faith, for Delhi telling us what Bengal should be. My vote is for Bengal to remain itself, imperfect and corrupt as it is, rather than be remade by a Centre that respects no rules and no constitutional limits.
primary_drivers: Bengal's autonomy and independence from Delhi's machinery
Asymmetrical, weaponized justice by ED-CBI targeting only opposition
Preventing the CAA and Hindutva remaking of Bengal's secular, syncretic culture
